text: The surgeon uses the lower pole of the kidney and the intersection with the iliac vessels as anatomical markers to achieve a precise overlay of the virtual ureter model onto the real ureter during the procedure.

In the first case, the surgeon uses augmented reality to superimpose a virtual model of the ureter and embedded stone over the operative field. The stricture is precisely located, and the ureter is incised to resect the stenotic tract. Ureteroscopy is performed to confirm the accuracy of the virtual model alignment.

In the second case, the surgeon prepares an omental flap for reconstruction. Using augmented reality, the virtual model of the ureter and stone is superimposed onto the real anatomy. The stone-related stricture in the middle portion of the upper ureter is identified, and the ureter is incised and resected, preserving a significant amount of healthy peristenotic ureter. The embedded stone is visibly detectable during the resection.
In the third case, the surgeon identifies the stricture located just below the ureteropelvic junction, with visible microcalcifications on the ureteral wall.

Indocyanine green (ICG) is injected through the nephrostomy, but it does not provide clear feedback on the stricture level. The ureter is visibly cut and spatulated, marking the beginning of the reconstructive phase.
The surgeon prepares a buccal mucosal graft by contouring a three by one centimeter section of oral mucosa. The graft is then cut using forceps in preparation for the robotic phase of the surgery.
The surgeon performs the fetting of the buccal mucosal graft, placing it into the abdominal cavity after completing the posterior plate of the anastomosis with a terminal-terminal suture.

The surgeon applies the mucosal graft over the ureteral defect, suturing it to the peripheral free margins of the anastomized ureteral segment. The graft tissue is carefully modeled on the defect until complete repair is achieved, allowing the progression of surgical instruments through the newly calibrated ureter.
The surgeon places the omental flap around the repaired ureter to ensure vascularization of the graft, aiming to prevent stricture recurrence.
